description: End-to-end validation with a real WC project (any project with a custom-elements.json will do — Lit, Stencil, FAST, or hand-coded). Install wc-mcp from the local pack, configure .mcp.json pointing at the project's custom-elements.json, connect to Claude Code or Claude Desktop. Manually verify: list_components returns the expected components, get_component returns full metadata for one component, find_component with a natural language query returns the right match, score_component returns a health score, diff_cem on main shows no breaking changes. Document any discrepancies. This is the acceptance gate before publish.
title: Smoke test wc-mcp with a real web component project

descriptionHistory.description: End-to-end validation: install wc-mcp (from local pack or npm) into the Helix project, configure .mcp.json pointing at Helix custom-elements.json, connect it to Claude Desktop or Claude Code, and manually verify: list_components returns all 14 hx-* components, get_component hx-button returns full metadata, find_component with a natural language query returns the right component, score_component returns a health score, diff_cem on main branch shows no breaking changes. Document any discrepancies found. This is the acceptance test that proves the tool works in a real-world scenario before publishing. Must pass before npm publish is approved.

End-to-end validation with a real WC project (any project with a custom-elements.json will do — Lit, Stencil, FAST, or hand-coded). Install wc-mcp from the local pack, configure .mcp.json pointing at the project's custom-elements.json, connect to Claude Code or Claude Desktop. Manually verify: list_components returns the expected components, get_component returns full metadata for one component, find_component with a natural language query returns the right match, score_component returns a health score, diff_cem on main shows no breaking changes. Document any discrepancies. This is the acceptance gate before publish.
